input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a MAUI `CollectionView` with single selection and pull-to-refresh:

**XAML:**

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:ItemModel">
                <Grid Padding="12" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                    <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="{Binding Status}" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel bindings needed:**

| Property/Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Items` (ObservableCollection) | Data source |
| `SelectedItem` | Two-way bound; set to `null` after navigation to deselect |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | Triggers navigation to detail page |
| `RefreshCommand` (AsyncRelayCommand) | Calls your API to reload data |
| `IsRefreshing` (bool) | Set `false` when refresh completes |

**ViewModel sketch (using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm):**

```csharp
public partial class ListViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty] ObservableCollection<ItemModel> items = new();
    [ObservableProperty] ItemModel? selectedItem;
    [ObservableProperty] bool isRefreshing;

    [RelayCommand]
    async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
        Items = new ObservableCollection<ItemModel>(data);
        IsRefreshing = false;
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    async Task SelectionChanged()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;
        await _nav.GoToAsync("DetailPage", new Dictionary<string, object>
        {
            ["Item"] = SelectedItem
        });
        SelectedItem = null; // deselect on return
    }
}
```

**Key points:**

1. **Wrap** `CollectionView` in `RefreshView` — the collection itself doesn't have pull-to-refresh.
2. **Reset `SelectedItem` to `null`** after navigating so the row is deselectable when the user comes back.
3. **Set `IsRefreshing = false`** at the end of your refresh logic or the spinner stays visible.
4. Use `SelectionMode="Single"` — for multi-select, switch to `Multiple` and bind `SelectedItems`.
